steps.title: Split hub file: template-helpers.js
steps.description: `src/agent-generator/templates/template-helpers.js` has 8 incoming connections. Split into 2 focused modules to reduce coupling.
steps.rationale: High fan-in (8 files depend on this) creates a bottleneck. Changes to this file ripple to 8 other files. Splitting by usage pattern reduces blast radius.

steps.title: Optimize barrel: src/index.ts
steps.description: `src/index.ts` re-exports 12 modules. This creates a "Shotgun Surgery" risk — any change propagates widely.
steps.rationale: Barrel files that re-export everything make it hard to tree-shake unused code and create implicit dependencies. Direct imports make dependency relationships explicit and reduce the blast radius of changes.

operations.description: Simplify index.ts: only re-export public API (11 re-exports detected, consider reducing)

steps.title: Reduce cross-boundary imports: template-generation.test.ts
steps.description: `tests/template-generation.test.ts` imports from 5 different modules: `src/agent-generator`, `src`, `src/agent-generator/templates/core`, `src/agent-generator/templates/domain`, `src/agent-generator/templates/stack`. Consider using a facade or dependency injection.
steps.rationale: Files with imports scattered across many modules have high afferent coupling. A facade centralizes these dependencies, making the file easier to test (mock one facade) and reducing the impact of changes in dependent modules.

steps.title: Group co-dependent files into `shared/`
steps.description: Files `types.js`, `types.js`, `template-helpers.js`, `base-detector.js`, `framework-registry.js`, `index.js`, `context-builder.js`, `index-md.js`, `orchestrator.js`, `preflight.js`, `quality-gates.js`, `general-rules.js`, `architecture-rules.js`, `security-rules.js`, `workflow-new-feature.js`, `workflow-fix-bug.js`, `workflow-review.js`, `agents.js`, `index.js`, `audit-engine.js`, `stack-detector.js`, `analysis-helpers.js`, `layer-classifier.js` are frequently imported together (co-import score: 79). Grouping them improves cohesion.
steps.rationale: Files that are frequently imported together belong in the same module. This improves discoverability and reduces the cognitive load of understanding which files work together.
